  "command": "curl -s http://localhost:3033/api/ukb/processes | python3 -c \"\nimport sys,json\nd=json.load(sys.stdin)\nprocs = d.get('data',{}).get('processes',[])\nprint(f'Processes found: {len(procs)}')\nfor p in procs:\n    print(f'  ID: {p.get(\\\"id\\\",\\\"?\\\")[:20]}  Status: {p.get(\\\"status\\\")}  Steps: {p.get(\\\"completedSteps\\\",0)}/{p.get(\\\"totalSteps\\\",0)}')\n    print(f'  stepPaused: {p.get(\\\"stepPaused\\\")}  singleStepMode: {p.get(\\\"singleStepMode\\\")}')\n    steps = p.get('steps', [])\n    print(f'  Steps detail: {len(steps)} entries')\n    for s in steps:\n        print(f'    - {s.get(\\\"name\\\")}: {s.get(\\\"status\\\")}  trace_fields: {bool(s.get(\\\"agentInstances\\\") or s.get(\\\"llmCallEvents\\\"))}')\n\" 2>&1",

}

{

}

**System Action:** (Initiated automatically)

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "curl -s http://localhost:3033/api/ukb/processes | python3 -c \"\nimport sys,json\nd=json.load(sys.stdin)\nprocs = d.get('data',{}).get('processes',[])\nprint(f'Processes found: {len(procs)}')\nfor p in procs:\n    print(f'  ID: {p.get(\\\"id\\\",\\\"?\\\")[:20]}  Status: {p.get(\\\"status\\\")}  Steps: {p.get(\\\"completedSteps\\\",0)}/{p.get(\\\"totalSteps\\\",0)}')\n    print(f'  stepPaused: {p.get(\\\"stepPaused\\\")}  singleStepMode: {p.get(\\\"singleStepMode\\\")}')\n    steps = p.get('steps', [])\n    print(f'  Steps detail: {len(steps)} entries')\n    for s in steps:\n        print(f'    - {s.get(\\\"name\\\")}: {s.get(\\\"status\\\")}  trace_fields: {bool(s.get(\\\"agentInstances\\\") or s.get(\\\"llmCallEvents\\\"))}')\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Check API response for gaps 1-4"
}
```

  ID: ?  Status: running  Steps: 0/4

{
  "command": "sleep 10 && docker exec coding-services cat /coding/.data/workflow-progress.json | python3 -c \"\nimport sys,json\nd=json.load(sys.stdin)\nprint(f'Status: {d.get(\\\"status\\\")}')\nprint(f'CurrentStep: {d.get(\\\"currentStep\\\")}')\nprint(f'StepPaused: {d.get(\\\"stepPaused\\\")}')\nprint(f'CompletedSteps: {d.get(\\\"completedSteps\\\", 0)}/{d.get(\\\"totalSteps\\\", 0)}')\nsd = d.get('stepsDetail', [])\nprint(f'StepsDetail: {len(sd)} entries')\nfor s in sd:\n    print(f'  - {s.get(\\\"name\\\")}: {s.get(\\\"status\\\")}  duration={s.get(\\\"duration\\\")}  tokensUsed={s.get(\\\"tokensUsed\\\")}  llmCalls={s.get(\\\"llmCalls\\\")}')\n    if s.get('agentInstances'):\n        print(f'    agents: {[a.get(\\\"agentName\\\") for a in s[\\\"agentInstances\\\"]]}')\n    if s.get('entityFlow'):\n        print(f'    entityFlow: {s[\\\"entityFlow\\\"]}')\n\"",
  "description": "Check if wave1 completed with trace data"
}
```

  "description": "Check if wave1 completed with trace data"
}

  "new_string": "                  const waveStatuses = [1, 2, 3, 4].map(waveNum => {\n                    // Check top-level wave step (e.g. \"wave1\") first\n                    const topLevel = steps.find((s: { name: string }) => s.name === `wave${waveNum}`)\n                    if (topLevel) return (topLevel as { status: string }).status === 'completed' ? 'completed'\n                      : (topLevel as { status: string }).status === 'running' ? 'running' : 'pending'\n                    // Fallback: derive from sub-steps (e.g. \"wave1_analyze\", \"wave1_classify\")\n                    const waveSteps = steps.filter((s: { name: string }) => s.name.startsWith(`wave${waveNum}_`))\n                    if (waveSteps.some((s: { status: string }) => s.status === 'running')) return 'running'\n                    if (waveSteps.length > 0 && waveSteps.every((s: { status: string }) => s.status === 'completed')) return 'completed'\n                    if (waveSteps.some((s: { status: string }) => s.status === 'completed')) return 'running'\n                    return 'pending'\n                  })"
}
```

}

}

  "new_string": "      // Initialize CGR query cache and start async index refresh\n      this.cgrCache = new CgrQueryCache(this.repositoryPath);\n      this.cgrBuilder = new CgrObservationBuilder();\n      if (isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath)) {\n        log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping CGR index refresh', 'info');\n      } else {\n        log('[WaveController] CGR index refresh started (30s timeout)', 'info');\n        await this.cgrCache.refreshIndex(30_000);\n      }\n      if (!this.cgrCache.isAvailable()) {\n        log('[WaveController] CGR unavailable -- continuing with LLM-only observations', 'warning');\n      }\n\n      this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_init', {\n        manifestEntries: flatEntries.length,\n        existingEntities: existingEntities.length,\n        cgrAvailable: this.cgrCache.isAvailable(),\n      });\n\n      // Pause BEFORE analyze (user sees \"about to analyze wave1\")\n      await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_analyze', true);\n      SemanticAnalyzer.resetStepMetrics();\n      dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_analyze', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n      let { result: wave1Result, agent: wave1Agent } = await this.executeWave1WithMetrics(manifest, existingEntities);"
}

  "new_string": "        // Pause BEFORE classify\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_classify', true);\n        SemanticAnalyzer.resetStepMetrics();\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_classify', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        const wave1ClassifyTasks = wave1Entities.map(entity => async () => {\n          await this.classifyEntity(entity);\n        });\n        await this.runWithConcurrency(wave1ClassifyTasks, 2);\n        this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_classify', { entitiesClassified: wave1Entities.length });\n        log('[WaveController] Wave 1 classification complete', 'info', { entities: wave1Entities.length });\n\n        // Pause BEFORE persist\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_persist', true);\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_persist', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        if (isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath)) {\n          const mockDelay = getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath);\n          await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, mockDelay));\n          log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping wave 1 persist', 'info');\n        } else {\n          await this.persistWaveResult(wave1Result);\n        }\n        this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_persist', { entitiesPersisted: wave1Result.totalEntities });\n        // Update entity flow: persisted count\n        this.captureEntityFlow('wave1_analyze', {\n          produced: wave1Entities.length,\n          passedQA: wave1Entities.length,\n          persisted: wave1Result.totalEntities,\n        });\n        log('[WaveController] Wave 1 entities persisted', 'info', {\n          entities: wave1Result.totalEntities,\n        });"

  "old_string": "        // Conv\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'operator_conv', wave: 3, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          const convStart = Date.now();\n          const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n          operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n          const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n          this.captureAgentMetrics('operator_conv', { providers: [], totalTokens: 0, totalCalls: 0 }, {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('operator_conv', true);",
  "new_string": "        // Mock mode: skip all KG operators (they do real graph work, not LLM calls)\n        const mockOperators = isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath);\n\n        // Conv\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('operator_conv', true);\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'operator_conv', wave: 3, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          if (mockOperators) {\n            await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath)));\n            log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping Conv operator', 'info');\n            operatorTimings.conv = getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath);\n          } else {\n            const convStart = Date.now();\n            const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n            currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n            operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n            const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n            log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n              inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n              withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n            });\n            this.captureAgentMetrics('operator_conv', { providers: [], totalTokens: 0, totalCalls: 0 }, {\n              inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n              withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n            });\n          }\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }"
}

{
  "command": "cd /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding && git add integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/ukb-workflow-graph.tsx integrations/system-health-dashboard/src/components/ukb-workflow-modal.tsx && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix(19.1): pre-execution pauses, mock bypass for non-LLM ops, green completed nodes\n\nThree fixes:\n\n1. Single-step pauses now fire BEFORE each sub-step executes (was after).\n   User sees \"about to run X\" instead of \"X already finished\". Eliminates\n   the visual jump where multiple sub-steps complete between clicks.\n\n2. Mock mode now bypasses non-LLM heavy operations: persistence, CGR\n   refresh, and all 6 KG operators (conv/aggr/embed/dedup/pred/merge)\n   plus direct graph write. Each skipped op uses mockDelay instead.\n\n3. Workflow graph nodes turn green when waves complete. Added WAVE_AGENTS\n   mapping so wave-level completed status propagates to agent node IDs.\n   Also added wave sub-step → agent STEP_TO_AGENT mappings and fixed\n   wave sidebar to check top-level wave step names (not just sub-steps).\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <<EMAIL_REDACTED>>\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit all fixes"
}
```

  "new_string": "      // Initialize CGR query cache and start async index refresh\n      this.cgrCache = new CgrQueryCache(this.repositoryPath);\n      this.cgrBuilder = new CgrObservationBuilder();\n      if (isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath)) {\n        log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping CGR index refresh', 'info');\n      } else {\n        log('[WaveController] CGR index refresh started (30s timeout)', 'info');\n        await this.cgrCache.refreshIndex(30_000);\n      }\n      if (!this.cgrCache.isAvailable()) {\n        log('[WaveController] CGR unavailable -- continuing with LLM-only observations', 'warning');\n      }\n\n      this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_init', {\n        manifestEntries: flatEntries.length,\n        existingEntities: existingEntities.length,\n        cgrAvailable: this.cgrCache.isAvailable(),\n      });\n\n      // Pause BEFORE analyze (user sees \"about to analyze wave1\")\n      await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_analyze', true);\n      SemanticAnalyzer.resetStepMetrics();\n      dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_analyze', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n      let { result: wave1Result, agent: wave1Agent } = await this.executeWave1WithMetrics(manifest, existingEntities);"
}

  "old_string": "        // Conv\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'operator_conv', wave: 3, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          const convStart = Date.now();\n          const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n          operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n          const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n          this.captureAgentMetrics('operator_conv', { providers: [], totalTokens: 0, totalCalls: 0 }, {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('operator_conv', true);",
  "new_string": "        // Mock mode: skip all KG operators (they do real graph work, not LLM calls)\n        const mockOperators = isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath);\n\n        // Conv\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('operator_conv', true);\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'operator_conv', wave: 3, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          if (mockOperators) {\n            await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath)));\n            log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping Conv operator', 'info');\n            operatorTimings.conv = getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath);\n          } else {\n            const convStart = Date.now();\n            const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n            currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n            operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n            const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n            log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n              inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n              withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n            });\n            this.captureAgentMetrics('operator_conv', { providers: [], totalTokens: 0, totalCalls: 0 }, {\n              inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n              withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n            });\n          }\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "<AWS_SECRET_REDACTED>ns/system-health-dashboard/src/components/ukb-workflow-graph.tsx",
  "old_string": "    return map\n    // Use stepsSignature instead of process.steps for reliable change detection\n    // stepsSignature changes when any step's name or status changes\n    // currentBatchSignature triggers recalc when new batch starts (resets batch-phase steps to pending)\n    // Also include _refreshKey and completedSteps for additional change detection\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [stepsSignature, currentBatchSignature, process.currentStep, process.completedSteps, STEP_TO_AGENT, (process as any)._refreshKey])",
  "new_string": "    // Wave-analysis: derive completed agents from currentStep position within the wave\n    // Each wave runs: analyze → classify → persist (with sub-steps mapping to agents)\n    // If currentStep is wave2_classify, then semantic_analysis is completed for this wave\n    const WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE = [\n      'semantic_analysis',   // waveN_init, waveN_analyze, sem_data_prep, sem_llm_analysis, sem_observation_gen, sem_entity_transform\n      'quality_assurance',   // waveN_qa\n      'ontology_classification', // waveN_classify\n      'persistence',         // waveN_persist\n      'kg_operators',        // operator_conv, operator_aggr, etc.\n      'insight_generation',  // wave4_insights\n    ]\n    if (process.currentStep) {\n      const currentAgentId = STEP_TO_AGENT[process.currentStep] || process.currentStep\n      const currentIdx = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.indexOf(currentAgentId)\n      if (currentIdx > 0) {\n        // All agents before current in the sequence are completed (within this wave)\n        for (let i = 0; i < currentIdx; i++) {\n          const priorAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          if (!map[priorAgent] || map[priorAgent].status === 'pending') {\n            map[priorAgent] = { name: priorAgent, status: 'completed' } as any\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return map\n    // Use stepsSignature instead of process.steps for reliable change detection\n    // stepsSignature changes when any step's name or status changes\n    // currentBatchSignature triggers recalc when new batch starts (resets batch-phase steps to pending)\n    // Also include _refreshKey and completedSteps for additional change detection\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [stepsSignature, currentBatchSignature, process.currentStep, process.completedSteps, STEP_TO_AGENT, (process as any)._refreshKey])"
}

  "new_string": "    // For batch workflows: ensure kg_operators uses current batch status (same as other multi-step agents)\n    if (isBatchWorkflow && currentBatchStepMap['kg_operators']) {\n      map['kg_operators'] = currentBatchStepMap['kg_operators']\n    }\n\n    // Wave-analysis: expand wave-level completed steps to agent nodes\n    // Each wave runs: analyze → classify → qa → persist (+ kg_operators)\n    // When wave1/wave2/etc are \"completed\", mark participating agents green\n    const WAVE_AGENTS: Record<string, string[]> = {\n      wave1: ['semantic_analysis', 'ontology_classification', 'quality_assurance', 'persistence', 'kg_operators'],\n      wave2: ['semantic_analysis', 'ontology_classification', 'quality_assurance', 'persistence', 'kg_operators'],\n      wave3: ['semantic_analysis', 'ontology_classification', 'quality_assurance', 'persistence', 'kg_operators'],\n      wave4: ['insight_generation', 'ontology_classification', 'quality_assurance', 'persistence', 'kg_operators'],\n    }\n    if (process.steps) {\n      for (const step of process.steps) {\n        const agents = WAVE_AGENTS[step.name]\n        if (agents) {\n          for (const agentId of agents) {\n            // Propagate wave status to agent nodes; running beats completed beats pending\n            const existing = map[agentId]\n            if (!existing || (step.status === 'completed' && existing.status !== 'running') || step.status === 'running') {\n              map[agentId] = { ...step, name: agentId, status: step.status as any }\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return map"
}

  "new_string": "      // Initialize CGR query cache and start async index refresh\n      this.cgrCache = new CgrQueryCache(this.repositoryPath);\n      this.cgrBuilder = new CgrObservationBuilder();\n      if (isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath)) {\n        log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping CGR index refresh', 'info');\n      } else {\n        log('[WaveController] CGR index refresh started (30s timeout)', 'info');\n        await this.cgrCache.refreshIndex(30_000);\n      }\n      if (!this.cgrCache.isAvailable()) {\n        log('[WaveController] CGR unavailable -- continuing with LLM-only observations', 'warning');\n      }\n\n      this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_init', {\n        manifestEntries: flatEntries.length,\n        existingEntities: existingEntities.length,\n        cgrAvailable: this.cgrCache.isAvailable(),\n      });\n\n      // Pause BEFORE analyze (user sees \"about to analyze wave1\")\n      await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_analyze', true);\n      SemanticAnalyzer.resetStepMetrics();\n      dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_analyze', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n      let { result: wave1Result, agent: wave1Agent } = await this.executeWave1WithMetrics(manifest, existingEntities);"
}

  "new_string": "        // Pause BEFORE classify\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_classify', true);\n        SemanticAnalyzer.resetStepMetrics();\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_classify', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        const wave1ClassifyTasks = wave1Entities.map(entity => async () => {\n          await this.classifyEntity(entity);\n        });\n        await this.runWithConcurrency(wave1ClassifyTasks, 2);\n        this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_classify', { entitiesClassified: wave1Entities.length });\n        log('[WaveController] Wave 1 classification complete', 'info', { entities: wave1Entities.length });\n\n        // Pause BEFORE persist\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('wave1_persist', true);\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'wave1_persist', wave: 1, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        if (isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath)) {\n          const mockDelay = getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath);\n          await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, mockDelay));\n          log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping wave 1 persist', 'info');\n        } else {\n          await this.persistWaveResult(wave1Result);\n        }\n        this.captureStepMetrics('wave1_persist', { entitiesPersisted: wave1Result.totalEntities });\n        // Update entity flow: persisted count\n        this.captureEntityFlow('wave1_analyze', {\n          produced: wave1Entities.length,\n          passedQA: wave1Entities.length,\n          persisted: wave1Result.totalEntities,\n        });\n        log('[WaveController] Wave 1 entities persisted', 'info', {\n          entities: wave1Result.totalEntities,\n        });"
}

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "<AWS_SECRET_REDACTED>ns/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/agents/wave-controller.ts",
  "old_string": "        // Conv\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'operator_conv', wave: 3, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          const convStart = Date.now();\n          const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n          operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n          const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n          this.captureAgentMetrics('operator_conv', { providers: [], totalTokens: 0, totalCalls: 0 }, {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('operator_conv', true);",
  "new_string": "        // Mock mode: skip all KG operators (they do real graph work, not LLM calls)\n        const mockOperators = isMockLLMEnabled(this.repositoryPath);\n\n        // Conv\n        await this.checkSingleStepPause('operator_conv', true);\n        dispatch({ type: 'substep-update', substepId: 'operator_conv', wave: 3, totalWaves: 4 });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          if (mockOperators) {\n            await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath)));\n            log('[WaveController] Mock mode: skipping Conv operator', 'info');\n            operatorTimings.conv = getMockDelay(this.repositoryPath);\n          } else {\n            const convStart = Date.now();\n            const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n            currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n            operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n            const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n            log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n              inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n              withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n            });\n            this.captureAgentMetrics('operator_conv', { providers: [], totalTokens: 0, totalCalls: 0 }, {\n              inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n              withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n            });\n          }\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }"
}

Each skipped op uses mockDelay instead.\n\n3. Workflow graph nodes turn green when waves complete. **User Request:** completed sub-steps are NOT marked green - nor are completed steps. Plus, the WF started on the first sub-step of step semantic (not the first step of the WF

**Tool:** Edit
**Input:** ```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "<AWS_SECRET_REDACTED>ns/system-health-dashboard/src/components/ukb-workflow-graph.tsx",
  "old_string": "    return map\n    // Use stepsSignature instead of process.steps for reliable change detection\n    // stepsSignature changes when any step's name or status changes\n    // currentBatchSignature triggers recalc when new batch starts (resets batch-phase steps to pending)\n    // Also include _refreshKey and completedSteps for additional change detection\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [stepsSignature, currentBatchSignature, process.currentStep, process.completedSteps, STEP_TO_AGENT, (process as any)._refreshKey])",
  "new_string": "    // Wave-analysis: derive completed agents from currentStep position within the wave\n    // Each wave runs: analyze → classify → persist (with sub-steps mapping to agents)\n    // If currentStep is wave2_classify, then semantic_analysis is completed for this wave\n    const WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE = [\n      'semantic_analysis',   // waveN_init, waveN_analyze, sem_data_prep, sem_llm_analysis, sem_observation_gen, sem_entity_transform\n      'quality_assurance',   // waveN_qa\n      'ontology_classification', // waveN_classify\n      'persistence',         // waveN_persist\n      'kg_operators',        // operator_conv, operator_aggr, etc.\n      'insight_generation',  // wave4_insights\n    ]\n    if (process.currentStep) {\n      const currentAgentId = STEP_TO_AGENT[process.currentStep] || process.currentStep\n      const currentIdx = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE.indexOf(currentAgentId)\n      if (currentIdx > 0) {\n        // All agents before current in the sequence are completed (within this wave)\n        for (let i = 0; i < currentIdx; i++) {\n          const priorAgent = WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE[i]\n          if (!map[priorAgent] || map[priorAgent].status === 'pending') {\n            map[priorAgent] = { name: priorAgent, status: 'completed' } as any\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return map\n    // Use stepsSignature instead of process.steps for reliable change detection\n    // stepsSignature changes when any step's name or status changes\n    // currentBatchSignature triggers recalc when new batch starts (resets batch-phase steps to pending)\n    // Also include _refreshKey and completedSteps for additional change detection\n    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps\n  }, [stepsSignature, currentBatchSignature, process.currentStep, process.completedSteps, STEP_TO_AGENT, (process as any)._refreshKey])"
}
```

  "new_string": "                        // Determine sub-step status from process.steps OR currentStep position\n                        const stepInfo = process.steps?.find(s => s.name === substep.id)\n                        let substepStatus = stepInfo?.status || 'pending'\n                        // For wave-analysis: derive from currentStep position within agent's sub-steps\n                        if (substepStatus === 'pending' && process.currentStep && agentSubSteps) {\n                          const agentSubs = agentSubSteps[agent.id]\n                          if (agentSubs) {\n                            const currentSubIdx = agentSubs.findIndex(s => s.id === process.currentStep)\n                            const thisSubIdx = agentSubs.findIndex(s => s.id === substep.id)\n                            if (currentSubIdx >= 0 && thisSubIdx >= 0) {\n                              if (thisSubIdx < currentSubIdx) substepStatus = 'completed'\n                              else if (thisSubIdx === currentSubIdx) substepStatus = 'running'\n                            } else if (currentSubIdx < 0 && thisSubIdx >= 0) {\n                              // currentStep maps to this agent but isn't a sub-step ID — check via STEP_TO_AGENT\n                              const currentAgent = STEP_TO_AGENT[process.currentStep] || process.currentStep\n                              if (currentAgent === agent.id) {\n                                // All sub-steps before current are completed\n                                const parentStatus = stepStatusMap[agent.id]?.status\n                                if (parentStatus === 'completed') substepStatus = 'completed'\n                              }\n                            }\n                          }\n                        }"
}

Always verify visually or with Playwright tests.\ntype: feedback\n---\n\n## Workflow Graph Coloring and Naming\n\n**Recurring frustration (10+ times asked):**\n- Completed steps/sub-steps MUST turn green in the workflow graph diagram\n- The old \"Batch Scheduler\" node was renamed to \"Data Preparation\" (Prep) — NEVER revert this\n- Sub-step arcs around agent nodes must also turn green when completed\n\n**Root causes fixed (2026-03-13):**\n- API only sends 4 wave-level steps (`wave1`-`wave4`), not individual sub-steps\n- Graph derives agent node status from: (1) wave completion propagation via `WAVE_AGENTS`, (2) `currentStep` position in `WAVE_SUBSTEP_SEQUENCE`\n- Sub-step arc status derived from `currentStep` position within `AGENT_SUBSTEPS[agent.id]`\n- `wave1_init` maps to `batch_scheduler` (Data Prep node)\n\n**CRITICAL: Always write Playwright tests when touching graph coloring/status logic.**\nUser explicitly requested automated tests so they don't have to manually verify this again.\n\n**Test should verify:**\n1. Start debug workflow, advance through steps\n2. Completed agent nodes have green fill (`#166534` fill, `#15803d` stroke)\n3. Completed sub-step arcs have green fill (`#22c55e`)\n4. Running node has green fill with bold stroke (strokeWidth 4)\n5. Pending nodes stay grey (`#f9fafb` fill)\n6. \"Data Preparation\" label appears (not \"Batch\")\n"
}
```

Visual verification that all fixes work together end-to-end\n3. ## Prompt Set (ps_1773384519457)

**Time:** 2026-03-13T06:48:39.457Z
**Duration:** 0ms
**Tool Calls:** 1

### ToolSearch - 2026-03-13 06:48:39 UTC [07:48:39 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** ToolSearch
**Input:** ```json
{
  "query": "select:Bash,Glob,Read",
  "max_results": 3
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
[
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Bash"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Glob"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Read"
  }
]
```


---

---

### ToolSearch - 2026-03-13 06:48:39 UTC [07:48:39 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** ToolSearch
**Input:** ```json
{
  "query": "select:Bash,Glob,Read",
  "max_results": 3
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
[
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Bash"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Glob"
  },
  {
    "type": "tool_reference",
    "tool_name": "Read"
  }
]
```


---

- "all": Load all files from today

- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** ToolSearch
**Input:** ```json
{
  "query": "select:Bash,Glob,Read",
  "max_results": 3
}
```

### Bash - 2026-03-13 06:48:39 UTC [07:48:39 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -lt /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -20",
  "description": "List recent LSL files sorted by modification time"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```

5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-13_0700-0800_c197ef.md",
  "limit": 150,
  "offset": 1
}
```

### Bash - 2026-03-13 06:48:39 UTC [07:48:39 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "wc -l /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-13_0700-0800_c197ef.md",

}

4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:

   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -lt /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -20",
  "description": "List recent LSL files sorted by modification time"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
total 851544
-rw-r--r-- 1 <USER_ID_REDACTED> staff   811667 Mar 13 07:49 2026-03-13_0700-0800_c197ef.md
-rw-r--r-- 1 <USER_ID_REDACTED> staff   256019 Mar 13 07:44 2026-03-13_0600-0700_c197ef.md
-rw-r--r-- 1 <USER_ID_REDACTED> staff   161902 Mar 13 07:40 2026-03-13_0700-0800_c197ef_from-rapid-automations.md
-rw-r--r-- 1 <USER_ID_REDACTED> staff    10440 Mar 13 07:00 2026-03-13_0600-0700_c197ef_from-rapid-automations.md
-rw-r--r-- 1 <USER_ID_REDACTED> staff   165859 Mar 11 19:02 2026-03-11_1800-1900_c197ef.md
-rw-r--r-- 1 <USER_ID_REDACTED> staff   209257 Mar 1
...[truncated]
```

### Read - 2026-03-13 06:48:39 UTC [07:48:39 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-13_0700-0800_c197ef.md",
  "offset": 200,
  "limit": 300
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```

   201→

   205→

   207→
   208→**Tool:** mcp__semantic-analysis__execute_workflow
   209→**Input:** ```json
   210→{
   211→  "workflow_name": "wave-analysis",
   212→  "async_mode": true,
   213→  "debug": true,
   214→  "parameters": {
   215→    "team": "coding",
   216→    "singleStepMode": true,
   217→    "mockLLM": tru
...[truncated]
```

### Read - 2026-03-13 06:48:39 UTC [07:48:39 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-13_0700-0800_c197ef.md",
  "offset": 21000,
  "limit": 500
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```

 21003→**User Request:** not working. The very first step (STILL called Batch - change this to something useful already. I asked for this 10 times already!!) ... this first step turns "greyed out/transparent" after being stepped over. Correct behavior would be to turn it green. Also, the completed sub-steps in semantics (and presumably all sub-steps) remain light-blue (= "not yet executed") as opposed to green (= done). Fi
...[truncated]
```
